article_meta.title: Family Welcome To The Future, Where AI Grandma Raises The Kid You Bought From The Baby Bank By: Elle Purnell November 19

reasoning: The summary consistently attributes evaluative language and arguments to the author, avoiding adoption of the source’s loaded framing. It quotes charged phrases with clear attribution and maintains neutral verbs. One minor interpretive claim about online criticism appears but does not dominate.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs like critiques, argues, contends; no editorial verbs adopted.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Notes 'widespread criticism online' without concrete sourcing beyond general references; other claims are attributed.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative points are framed as the author's views (argues, contends, frames).
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Includes charged phrases only in quotes and with attribution; tone remains measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No speculation about intentions without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Focus aligns with the op-ed’s perspective but adds mention of the ad and sources; minimal imbalance.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures key perspectives (author, 2wai, CNA, Bloomberg, SNL); minor items like specific study authors not listed.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Avoids subjective adjectives in the model’s own voice.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral vocabulary; ideological terms appear only as the author’s framing.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects source tone via attribution without amplifying negativity.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No systematic favoring of elite or grassroots sources.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Materially more neutral than the source; charged claims are distanced.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Includes major perspectives referenced; some secondary citations not exhaustively listed.
